content:
That's exactly how the tool works — I searched for **R-26-33-53-07** and found **nothing in the ledger**. The tool never fabricates. If an ID, clause, or part number isn't in the parsed documents, I say so plainly.

**Now try a real question.** Here are a few to get started — pick one:

| If you ask... | I'll return... |
|---|---|
| *"What does spec 26 33 53 say about harmonic filters?"* | Verbatim text from `spec_26_33_53.pdf` with page cites |
| *"Show me the deviation queue."* | Open deviations sorted by severity from the queue page |
| *"What changed in addendum 3?"* | Blast register — each rule/requirement that addendum 3 altered |
| *"Status of SUB-261326-01-R0?"* | Its review state, linked clauses, and reviewer comments |
| *"Which vendors are flagged on the trust ledger?"* | Per-vendor trust scores and open issues |
| *"What's the facility rating and redundancy?"* | Tier III profile, N+1/N+N, relevant standards |

What would you like to know?
reasoning_content: Great. So the ledger is loaded with 22 documents, 424 rules, etc. The ID R-26-33-53-07 doesn't exist, so I demonstrated exactly what happens when a fact isn't in the ledger. Now let me answer the user.
